title: Oreobroma columbianum
intro: Oreobroma columbianum, also known as Colombian groundsel, is a flowering plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, which can help to soothe skin irritations, boost the immune system, and combat infections. The bioactive constituents of Oreobroma columbianum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often employed to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to support digestive health.
